title: Restrict BYOP redirect URI schemes
author: its3li
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- restrict BYOP redirect URIs to `https://` by default, with `http://` allowed only for loopback hosts
- apply the same redirect-scheme policy in allowlist matching, app-key creation, and metadata updates
- add regression coverage for app creation, metadata updates, stale stored metadata, app lookup, and secret-key minting

## Testing
- `XDG_CONFIG_HOME=F:\tmp\xdg-config npx vitest run test/url-utils.test.ts test/integration/api-keys.test.ts test/integration/account-keys.test.ts`
- `XDG_CONFIG_HOME=F:\tmp\xdg-config npm run typecheck`

##  TODO before merge

- [ ] **CI green** — all four checks currently `queued`, wait for them to complete.
- [ ] **Migration audit (prod D1).** Confirm no existing publishable keys have registered redirect URIs that this PR will retroactively reject. Run against prod:
  ```sql
  SELECT id, name, metadata
  FROM apikey
  WHERE metadata LIKE '%redirectUris%'
    AND metadata NOT LIKE '%"https://%'
    AND metadata NOT LIKE '%"http://127.0.0.1%'
    AND metadata NOT LIKE '%"http://localhost%'
    AND metadata NOT LIKE '%"http://[::1]%';
```

> If 0 rows: safe to merge as-is. If >0 rows: list owners and decide — notify + grace period, grandfather via flag, or force-update.
- [ ]  Custom URL schemes. Confirm no registered native app relies on a private-use scheme (com.example.app://). RFC 8252 §7.1 allows these; this PR rejects them. Same query as above, looking for redirectUris values that don't start with http.

gist.headline: Locking down the flight path
gist.blurb: We've tightened up security for BYOP apps by ensuring redirect URIs use secure HTTPS connections or local loopbacks. Keeping the hive safe from misdirected pollen!
gist.summary: BYOP redirect URIs are now restricted to https:// by default, with http:// permitted only for local loopback addresses across app-key creation and metadata updates.
gist.impact: Developers configuring BYOP apps must now use secure HTTPS endpoints or localhost for their redirect URIs, preventing potential vulnerabilities from unencrypted or custom scheme redirects.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations.ai bee acting as a friendly traffic controller, directing glowing data packets along a secure, glowing green path. A chunky robot companion holds a 'HTTPS' sign.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny traffic controller hat, directing glowing data packets along a bright lime green (#ecf874) path. A chunky robot companion with a CRT monitor head holds a sign reading 'HTTPS' in pixel font. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
